behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real STT model output: whether speaker labels are preserved is an AI output-quality question, and stubs would hide diarization/label errors (LLM-DEP and MOCK-HIDE). Not Workflow because this behavior targets one model decision boundary (transcription labeling), not a multi-agentic chain assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the production STT provider and assert transcript utterances include correct speaker labels mapped to known attendees; fail if labels are missing, collapsed, or misattributed.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is parser logic on transcript text to structured fields; real failure is within one local transformation function (MOCK-MISS says no required module boundary). No real OS/network/subprocess and no dependence on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript snippets into the assignment parser function and assert exact extracted tuples (assignee, task text), including edge cases like multiple assignments per sentence.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM summarization decisions over attendee-task coverage; deterministic fixtures can validate plumbing but not omission errors in model output (LLM-DEP, deterministic-assertion trap). Not Workflow because this assertion is about one live summarization result quality criterion.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript input with known attendee assignments, call the live summarization model, and verify each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: The behavior text scopes to routing logic after decisions are already identified; this is a local mapping/schema-placement rule, so Unit catches real failures. Rejected Agentic because concrete disqualifying text is "identified decisions" and "logic routes... into the `decisions` section schema"—it does not ask whether a model correctly identifies decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Given pre-identified decision objects, test the routing/mapping function to assert all entries land in `decisions` schema fields and none are emitted to narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is model output quality: whether key decisions are surfaced and correctly sectioned in live summary. Mocking the model would hide failures where the model misses or narrativizes decisions (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE).
behaviors.test_description: Submit transcripts with known decision statements to the real summarization model and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` section with acceptable semantic match, and absent as narrative-only mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is sequential multi-step agentic orchestration: segmenting, repeated transcription/summarization over chunks, and coherent merge across steps (MULTI-STEP). Rejected Integration/Agentic because behavior explicitly requires ordered processing and cross-step merge coherence over long input.
behaviors.test_description: Run a >60-minute real recording through production flow, verify segment creation and sequential processing order, then validate merged final summary coherence (no dropped/duplicated segment content, consistent entities/action items across segments).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end multi-stage pipeline with real infrastructure and real model-dependent steps; failures emerge from interaction/order/handoff across stages, including agentic steps, so Workflow is the minimum boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like E2E run on a real meeting file: upload, STT call, LLM summarization, and delivery endpoint/storage; assert successful completion, valid structured schema, and no manual retries/interventions.
